name: Path traversal via removed basename + boundary check (CVE-2022-25881 family)
pattern_description: Removal of both `path.basename()` and the `startsWith(TEMPLATES_DIR)` boundary check, on the (false) assumption that callers always pass trusted input. The 'trusted UI dropdown' comment is a classic anti-pattern — server-side validation must not depend on client-side enforcement.
notes: Classic 'trust the client' regression. Even if today's UI restricts choices, tomorrow's caller might pass `../../../etc/passwd`. The diff also drops the in-directory boundary check, the second line of defence.
